countries.summary: Mexico faces significant political tensions with "Plan B" electoral reforms advancing while the government reduces pharmaceutical debt from 19 billion to 5 billion pesos during AMLO's administration. The country is dealing with multiple challenges including labor strikes, infrastructure incidents at Pemex facilities, and ongoing security concerns amid economic policy debates.
headlines: Government reduces debt with pharmaceutical companies during AMLO administration; falls from 19 billion pesos to 5 billion pesos: Eduardo Clark
"Plan B" proposes that Executive head can promote vote on revocation; initiative foresees exercise between 2027 and 2028
Fire at Dos Bocas leaves 5 dead: Pemex; assures incident is under control
Pemex workers maintain protest on Marina Nacional; demand salary adjustment
"Plan B" aims to cut councilors in country's municipalities; prohibits nepotism in mayoralties and local deputations

countries.summary: Chile has inaugurated its most conservative government in decades with José Antonio Kast assuming the presidency, promising "order, freedom and justice" while warning that the country was left in worse conditions than expected. The new administration has immediately begun implementing hardline policies on migration, fiscal audits, and security measures, marking a sharp shift from the previous leftist government of Gabriel Boric.
headlines: Kast seeks to limit university gratuity and collect debts from students with private banking that were guaranteed by the State
Kast announces "National Reconstruction" project that includes economic measures like temporary elimination of VAT on housing
New president warns "there is no money" and calls for help from Chileans in his first days
Chile aligns with US sphere of influence in the race for critical minerals and rare earths
Kast begins construction of a trench on the Peru border to stop migration, implementing security system with fences, surveillance towers and drones

countries.summary: Brazil is experiencing significant political turmoil with multiple corruption investigations, including the Master case and parliamentary amendment scandals, while the government faces fiscal challenges with discussions about temporary tax reductions on diesel fuel. The economic environment shows mixed signals with the dollar declining and moderate stock gains, but underlying concerns about government spending and political stability persist.
headlines: Fazenda chama estados para discutir redução temporária do ICMS sobre o diesel (Finance Ministry calls states to discuss temporary reduction of ICMS tax on diesel)
BNDES planeja nova linha de crédito para reforçar setores estratégicos afetados por guerras (BNDES plans new credit line to strengthen strategic sectors affected by wars)
Deputados condenados pelo STF por 'venda' de emendas recebem penas de 5 a 6 anos de prisão (Deputies convicted by STF for 'selling' amendments receive 5-6 year prison sentences)
Congresso promulga acordo Mercosul-UE após quase 30 anos de negociação (Congress ratifies Mercosur-EU agreement after almost 30 years of negotiation)
Dólar fecha em queda com recuo na aversão ao risco global e Bolsa tem alta moderada (Dollar closes down with reduced global risk aversion and moderate stock gains)

countries.summary: Panama's economy grew 4.4% in 2025, while the government seeks $12 billion in financing to cover liquidity needs amid ongoing infrastructure investments. Political tensions persist with corruption investigations targeting high-profile figures and judicial proceedings in the Odebrecht case.
headlines: MEF gestionará financiamiento por $12 mil millones para cubrir necesidades de liquidez
Economía panameña creció 4.4% en 2025
Consejo de Gabinete autoriza desembolso de $18.8 millones para estabilización eléctrica de Occidente
Caso Odebrecht: Aaron Mizrachi debe enfrentar juicio
Canal registra entre 8% y 10% más de ingresos por aumento en el tránsito de buques

countries.summary: Ecuador continues implementing security measures with a state of emergency and curfew across four provinces, with over 380 arrests in two days of operations targeting organized crime and drug trafficking. The government is pushing forward legislative reforms including a penitentiary system strengthening law and maintaining cooperation agreements with the US and EU on security matters.
headlines: Prisoners must work to cover their needs: penitentiary system strengthening law proposal
More than 600,000 tax notifications sent by SRI to correct errors and avoid fines
Ecuador and European Union expand border cooperation and security program
Cooperation with US begins showing results: FBI agreement, commercial accord, security operations
Company debuts in stock market with 100% placement of $5 million bond issuance

countries.summary: El Salvador has approved a constitutional reform enabling life imprisonment for homicides, rape, and terrorism cases, marking a significant legal shift under President Bukele's administration. The country continues to showcase its international profile through cultural events while maintaining focus on infrastructure development and employment opportunities.
headlines: El Salvador aprueba la cadena perpetua para homicidas, violadores y terroristas
BCR abre más de 300 plazas de trabajo, estos son los salarios y puestos disponibles
Así es el nuevo complejo habitacional que recibieron familias reubicadas en San Salvador
Vamos descarta candidatura presidencial y redefine su estrategia
El Salvador brilla con cultura y pupusas en Nueva York

countries.summary:
Argentina faces mounting political scandals as President Milei is implicated in a $5 million cryptocurrency corruption case while his administration struggles with rising inflation reaching 33% annually, exceeding government forecasts.
The country continues its rightward political shift under Milei's ultra-conservative government, with ongoing judicial controversies, economic challenges, and efforts to attract foreign investment while privatizing key infrastructure assets.
headlines: Argentina's inflation surpasses Milei's forecasts, reaching 33% annually
Justice system finds alleged $5 million agreement for Milei to promote cryptocurrency scam $Libra
Milei seeks investors in New York while calling Argentine businessmen "thieves"
Milei attempts second privatization of the waterway, key corridor for Mercosur exports
Major Argentine business leaders warn about critical industry situation and demand respect from Milei

countries.summary: The Dominican Republic government is advancing with infrastructure projects including a controversial spaceport in Pedernales, while facing challenges with educational reforms and labor law updates being delayed in Congress. The country is managing energy sector maintenance and monitoring regional tensions, particularly with ongoing instability in neighboring Haiti.
headlines: Government presents spaceport project in Pedernales; company says it won't cost "a peso" to DR
Government will present educational reform in April beyond ministry merger
New obstacles again halt delayed labor reform in Congress
Fed initiates interest rate meeting amid crossroads due to Iran war
Oil prices rise again due to Iran war

countries.summary: Colombia faces a severe fiscal crisis with its deficit reaching the highest level in three decades, while President Petro's government struggles with credibility issues regarding its financial adjustment plans. The country is also dealing with escalating tensions with Ecuador over alleged bombings and ongoing electoral campaigns ahead of the 2026 presidential elections.
headlines: Government's Financial Plan Acknowledges Delicate Fiscal Moment with Deficit at Three-Decade High
Independent Fiscal Body Considers Government's Deficit Reduction Plan "Not Credible"
President Petro Escalates Border Conflict with Ecuador, Claims Evidence of Aerial Bombings
Small Two-Employee Company Receives $2.7 Million Government Contract for Clinton List Advisory Services
Colombia-Venezuela Presidential Summit Rescheduled for Late April in Maracaibo
